message:
perf(mvcc): gate visibility_ranges side-index off version-chain append hot path

Every `VersionStore::publish()` call — i.e., every committed page-version
append — unconditionally took `RwLock<HashMap<VersionIdx,
VersionVisibilityRange>>::write()` and did one `HashMap::insert` plus a
best-effort `HashMap::get_mut` to retro-close the previous head's
`end_ts`. Two GC paths (`gc_tick` + `prune_page_chain_eager`) also took
the same write lock to remove entries.

But nobody reads the side-index on a production path:

  grep -r '\.visibility_range(' in fsqlite-* →
    invariants.rs:1998-2000 (the one diagnostic test)
    nothing else in the tree

The `Debug` impl reads `ranges.len()` for pretty-printing and that's it.
So the publish/GC writes were pure dead weight on the write hot path.

Add `MVCC_VISIBILITY_RANGES_TRACKING_ENABLED: AtomicBool` (default
`false`) and new `pub fn set_mvcc_visibility_ranges_tracking_enabled` /
`mvcc_visibility_ranges_tracking_enabled` wrappers, matching the
`MVCC_SNAPSHOT_METRICS_ENABLED` gate pattern landed in bc4fa6b5.
`publish()` now short-circuits on one relaxed bool load when tracking is
off; the recording body moves into a `#[cold] #[inline(never)]`
`publish_record_visibility_range` so the common path is a predicted-
taken single-branch. Both GC cleanup loops gain the same gate check
(empty map on disabled → nothing to remove).

The one existing test that asserts the side-index behaviour
(`test_version_visibility_ranges_track_begin_end_timestamps`) flips the
gate on before its `publish()` calls; no other test fails. The field
and the `visibility_range()` / `Debug` readers stay so existing tests
and diagnostics still work when the gate is flipped on.

Before: 2582 ns/publish (tracking on — the pre-commit production state)
After:  2364 ns/publish (tracking off — the new default)
Delta:  -8.5% per version-chain append; sign consistent across all 9
        paired trials (every tracking-off sample faster than every
        tracking-on sample in the same trial)
Bench:  bench_publish_visibility_ranges_gate (added here; 32 pages,
        20000 publishes/trial, n=9 trials)
Cmd:    cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --release \
        bench_publish_visibility_ranges_gate -- --ignored --nocapture

Correctness: 40 `invariants::tests` pass, including
`test_version_visibility_ranges_track_begin_end_timestamps` (flips the
gate on), `test_theorem5_version_chain_bounded`, and the
`loom_chain_head_publication_linearizable` loom test.

Bead: bd-z9kx0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
